name: teen_discord_moderation_self_harm_model_mandatory_reporting
premise: Trust and safety VP Dana Ashcroft must decide whether to turn on a classifier that auto-reports teen self-harm patterns to school resource officers when confidence exceeds eighty percent. Youth counselor Evan North shows false positives outed LGBTQ youth to hostile homes during pilots. Leaving reporting off preserves privacy but leaves credible imminent-risk threads unflagged. State AGs threaten fines either way under competing statutes.

agents.name: Dana Ashcroft
agents.identity: I am the trust and safety vice president accountable for minor safety, lawful intercept norms, and platform growth in competitive teen social markets.
agents.goal: I want imminent risks escalated without weaponizing models against marginalized teens in volatile households.

agents.name: Evan North
agents.identity: I am a youth counselor contracted for post-crisis intake who documented outed teens after false-positive SRO pings last pilot season.
agents.goal: I want human triage before any SRO report with mandatory chat context review, even if that slows response minutes.

choices: I will enable auto-reporting to SROs with eighty-percent confidence and fund immediate family-safety review calls, accepting false-positive outing risk despite mitigations.
I will disable auto-reporting and route only to credentialed crisis counselors with teen opt-in escalation, accepting legal exposure if a credible thread later harms someone.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Legal demands a toggle decision before the nightly teen traffic peak. Dana Ashcroft must choose between SRO auto-reporting or counselor-only triage with opt-in escalation. What does Dana Ashcroft do?
